content.problemStatement:
Magic is really very simple, all you’ve got to do is want something and then let yourself have it.

Chadda and his Wizard friend PSC were exploring the enchanted forest on Halloween, when Chadda stumbled upon an array AAA of NNN magical numbers which took him into a different world.

Chadda remembered that PSC gave him two integers PPP and QQQ for such a situation.

Using these integers, Chadda can modify the array AAA as follows:


**At most** PPP times, perform the following operation:

Pick two elements xxx and yyy from AAA, delete them both from AAA, and insert (x+y)(x+y)(x+y) into AAA.

This operation can be performed only if AAA has at least two elements.


**At most** QQQ times, perform the following operation:

Pick two elements xxx and yyy from AAA, delete them both from AAA, and insert (x−y)(x-y)(x−y) into AAA.

This operation can also be performed only if AAA has at least two elements.


Note that each operation reduces the size of AAA by one.

The two types of operations (addition and subtraction) can be performed in any order, as long as at most PPP addition operations and QQQ subtraction operations are made.

Let BBB denote the final array obtained after performing some (possibly, zero) operations.

To return to his original world, Chadda has to find the **maximum possible** value of

max⁡(B)−min⁡(B)\max(B) - \min(B)max(B)−min(B)across all possible final arrays BBB.

Can you help Chadda find this value?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the size of the array.
The second line contains two space-separated integers PPP and QQQ — the maximum number of addition and subtraction operations, respectively.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​: the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the maximum possible value of max⁡(B)−min⁡(B)\max(B) - \min(B)max(B)−min(B) across all possible final arrays BBB.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^5 3⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: P=Q=0P = Q = 0P=Q=0, so no operations can be performed at all.
The answer is just max⁡([5,1])−min⁡([5,1])=5−1=4\max([5, 1]) - \min([5, 1]) = 5-1 = 4max([5,1])−min([5,1])=5−1=4.
Test case 222: The array is A=[8,−1,−4,2,6,−3]A = [8, -1, -4, 2, 6, -3]A=[8,−1,−4,2,6,−3]. The following sequence of operations can be performed:

Choose 222 and −3-3−3, remove them, and insert 2−(−3)=52 - (-3) = 52−(−3)=5 into the array.
The elements are now [8,−1,−4,6,5][8, -1, -4, 6, 5][8,−1,−4,6,5].
Choose 888 and 555, remove them, and add 8+5=138+5 = 138+5=13 to the array.
The elements are now [13,−1,−4,6][13, -1, -4, 6][13,−1,−4,6].
Choose −4-4−4 and 666, remove them and add (−4)−(6)(-4) - (6)(−4)−(6) to the array.
The elements are now [13,−1,−10][13, -1, -10][13,−1,−10].

The difference between maximum and minimum for this array is 13−(−10)=2313 - (-10) = 2313−(−10)=23.
With one addition and two subtraction operations available, it can be proved that this is the maximum attainable value.
Test Case 333:: It can be proven that 151515 is the maximum attainable value.
